blocks.content: Five seconds. Seven. Long enough for it to become a thing that people notice, and then people start noticing each other noticing.
I found a table near the back wall. Nobody I knew. I just — sat. Put my food down and sat like I'd meant to end up there.

blocks.speaker: Kendall
blocks.content: He was looking for you. Like actively.

blocks.speaker: Kendall
blocks.content: I can see that. You should probably go over there.

blocks.speaker: Kendall
blocks.content: That's his table though. The table.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: I know what table it is.
He shrugged and went back to his own lunch. At the center table Jonathan was mid-story, both hands moving, everyone leaning in. He didn't scan for me again.
Maya was at her table. She'd seen me standing in the middle of the room. The look on her face wasn't pity — it was something more precise than that.

blocks.text: is going to have to figure something out.

blocks.prompt: What does Larry do with the rest of lunch?

options.label: Watch Jonathan. Figure out how he makes it look easy.
Just eat. It's one lunch. It doesn't mean anything.
Look at your phone. Look busy. Don't let it show.
